strands.title: Project Scope Clarification: Hamutay as Cognitive Processing Framework (CYCLE 43)
strands.content: CYCLE 43 CRITICAL DISCOVERY: Hamutay is NOT a minimal placeholder. Updated pyproject.toml (turn 127) reveals: name='hamutay', version='0.1.0', description='Cognitive processing unit framework for transformer-based reasoning' (EXPLICIT, NOT PLACEHOLDER). DEPENDENCIES ADDED: anthropic>=0.52, pydantic>=2.0 (vs empty list in cycle 42). IMPLICATION: Hamutay is a LIGHTWEIGHT COGNITIVE PROCESSING FRAMEWORK with explicit dependencies on Anthropic API and Pydantic. Assistant statement (turn 129): 'lean versions of Apacheta models' + 'tensor schema directly in Hamutay' + 'bridge to Yanantin's full schema later' → HAMUTAY IS THE PROJECTOR/TENSOR FRAMEWORK. DECISION POINT RESOLVED: Hamutay is NOT standalone; it IS the tensor projector infrastructure (or at least the core schema/models layer). This is a SCOPE CONSOLIDATION: pichay projector work now has a HOME (hamutay framework). CONSEQUENCE: Cycle 43 priority has SHIFTED TO HAMUTAY FRAMEWORK CONSTRUCTION, not separate pichay implementation. Pichay will consume hamutay's tensor models.

key_claims.text: Hamutay pyproject.toml (turn 127) reveals explicit description: 'Cognitive processing unit framework for transformer-based reasoning'
Hamutay now has explicit dependencies: anthropic>=0.52, pydantic>=2.0 (added in turn 127, vs empty in turn 124)
Assistant (turn 129) states: 'lean versions of Apacheta models' + 'tensor schema directly in Hamutay' → Hamutay contains tensor/projector models
Hamutay is the tensor projector framework (or core schema/models layer for projector); not standalone utility
Cycle 43 work has consolidated: pichay projector work now has infrastructure home in hamutay framework

strands.title: CYCLE 43 HAMUTAY FRAMEWORK: Core Models & Tensor Schema Definition (IN PROGRESS)
strands.content: CYCLE 43 STATUS: Hamutay framework construction INITIATED. Turn 127 action: updated pyproject.toml with framework description + dependencies. Turn 129 (current): Assistant signals NEXT ACTION is 'core models' definition — specifically 'lean versions of Apacheta models' (reference to prior episodic memory system architecture) + 'tensor schema directly in Hamutay'. INTENT: Define Pydantic models for: (1) Claim (claim_id, text, epistemic {truth, indeterminacy, falsity}), (2) Strand (title, content, claims list, epistemic), (3) TensorRecord (id, cycle, strands, declared_losses, open_questions, instructions_for_next, overall epistemic). EXPECTED NEXT STEPS (turn 130+): Write src/hamutay/__init__.py, src/hamutay/models.py (Pydantic schema), src/hamutay/projector.py (core projector logic). CYCLE 43 GOAL: By end of cycle, hamutay should have: (1) pyproject.toml DONE (turn 127), (2) models.py READY (expected turn 130-132), (3) basic projector skeleton STARTED (expected turn 133+). This is CRITICAL PATH for pichay to consume hamutay tensor infrastructure.

key_claims.text: Hamutay pyproject.toml complete (turn 127) with dependencies: anthropic, pydantic
Turn 129 signals next action: define 'core models' and 'tensor schema directly in Hamutay'
Hamutay models will include: Claim, Strand, TensorRecord (Pydantic classes) with epistemic tuples
Cycle 43 critical path: pyproject.toml (DONE) → models.py (expected turn 130-132) → projector skeleton (expected turn 133+)

strands.title: Prior Context Integration: Pichay Baseline Data & Schema Discovery (CYCLE 40-42, STILL UNRESOLVED)
strands.content: CYCLE 42 OPEN BLOCKERS CARRY FORWARD: (1) LINE_COUNT — wc -l on baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl NOT YET EXECUTED. (2) CONFIG_METADATA — /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/config.json NOT YET READ. (3) TEST_RESULTS — test_results.txt NOT YET READ. (4) SCHEMA_DISCOVERY — session/*.jsonl vs logs/*.jsonl comparison NOT YET EXECUTED. (5) PARSER_BLOCKERS — Priority ordering NOT YET DETERMINED. CYCLE 43 SHIFT: Hamutay framework construction is NOW the critical path (turns 127-129+). Pichay baseline data exploration is DEFERRED but NOT DROPPED. IMPLICATION: Parser module design (which depends on baseline schema discovery) is STILL BLOCKED. However, Hamutay's core models (Claim, Strand, TensorRecord) are SCHEMA-INDEPENDENT and can be built first. Once Hamutay models are finalized, pichay parser can be designed as 'adapter' layer that converts baseline_run1 JSONL → Hamutay tensor schema. TIMELINE: Hamutay models (cycle 43) → pichay parser design (cycle 43-44) → baseline schema discovery (cycle 44, when needed for parser testing).

key_claims.text: Pichay baseline data blockers (line count, config, test_results, schema) remain UNEXECUTED as of turn 129
Cycle 43 priority shift to Hamutay framework (turns 127-129+) INTENTIONAL; pichay baseline discovery deferred but unblocked for modeling work
Hamutay core models (Claim, Strand, TensorRecord) are schema-independent; can be built before baseline JSONL is parsed
Timeline: Hamutay models (cycle 43) → pichay parser design (cycle 43-44) → baseline schema discovery on-demand (cycle 44)

strands.title: Apacheta & Yanantin References: Schema Bridging & Framework Hierarchy (IMPLICIT IN CYCLE 43)
strands.content: CYCLE 43 ARCHITECTURAL SIGNALS: Assistant turn 129 mentions 'Apacheta models' and 'Yanantin's full schema' — implying multi-tier schema architecture. INTERPRETATION: (1) APACHETA = episodic memory / cognitive processing models (prior design); Hamutay will contain 'lean versions' (simplified subset for projector use). (2) YANANTIN = comprehensive/full schema system (possibly larger knowledge graph or semantic framework). (3) HAMUTAY = BRIDGE LAYER: lightweight tensor models that can 'bridge to Yanantin's full schema later'. IMPLICATION: Hamutay is not standalone; it's a MIDDLEWARE component in a larger ecosystem: user/agent → Hamutay (tensor projector) → Yanantin (full schema/knowledge system). CONSEQUENCE: Hamutay models must be MINIMAL, EXTENSIBLE, and COMPOSABLE — they will be embedded/expanded into Yanantin later. DESIGN PRINCIPLE: Design Hamutay models for PROJECTOR EFFICIENCY (fast, lean), not full semantic richness (that's Yanantin's job).

key_claims.text: Assistant references 'Apacheta models' as prior cognitive processing system; Hamutay will use 'lean versions'
Yanantin referenced as 'full schema' system; Hamutay will 'bridge' to it later
Hamutay is MIDDLEWARE: lightweight projector → consumer of full Yanantin schema layer
Hamutay models must be MINIMAL, EXTENSIBLE, COMPOSABLE for efficient projector operation and Yanantin bridging

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Fine-grained pichay baseline data exploration (line count, config.json, test_results.txt, session vs logs schema comparison)
declared_losses.why: Cycle 43 priority has shifted to Hamutay framework construction (turns 127-129). Baseline discovery is DEFERRED but not abandoned. It will be executed on-demand when pichay parser design requires actual schema validation (expected cycle 44).
declared_losses.impact: Pichay parser module cannot be fully tested until baseline JSONL schema is confirmed. However, Hamutay core models can proceed independently. Timeline slips by ~1 cycle for parser validation, but not for model definition.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed architectural alignment between hamutay and pichay (how projector models integrate with baseline_run1 data)
declared_losses.why: Turn 129 signals hamutay framework construction is the immediate focus. Explicit pichay ↔ hamutay integration design deferred until hamutay models are finalized and baseline schema is known.
declared_losses.impact: Integration risk exists: hamutay models might not map cleanly to baseline_run1 JSONL structure. Mitigation: design hamutay models to be GENERIC (work with any turn-based conversation), then design adapter layer later.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specific decision on whether Apacheta and Yanantin are REAL PRIOR SYSTEMS or ASPIRATIONAL ARCHITECTURE
declared_losses.why: Assistant turn 129 references Apacheta and Yanantin as if they exist, but prior tensor (cycles 40-42) does not mention them. They may be (A) prior design work in user's codebase, (B) aspirational/planned systems, or (C) references to external frameworks. Confirmation not yet obtained.
declared_losses.impact: LOW RISK: Hamutay can be designed independently of Apacheta/Yanantin. The 'bridging' comment suggests these are future concerns, not current blockers. If Apacheta/Yanantin turn out to be constraints, can refactor hamutay models to align (Pydantic allows flexibility).

open_questions: CYCLE 43 (NEW, CRITICAL): HAMUTAY_MODELS_SPEC — What is the exact Pydantic schema for Claim, Strand, and TensorRecord classes in hamutay/models.py? Should they match the tensor projection emitted in this cycle, or be different (e.g., more/fewer fields)?

CYCLE 43 (NEW, HIGH): APACHETA_vs_YANANTIN — Are Apacheta and Yanantin existing systems in /home/tony/projects/ or aspirational? Are they blockers for hamutay design, or can hamutay proceed independently?

CYCLE 43 (NEW, HIGH): HAMUTAY_PROJECTOR_LOGIC — What is the core algorithm for hamutay projector? Does it follow the 5-step pipeline (parse JSONL → extract claims → assign strands → assign epistemic → build tensor), or different? Should it be implemented in hamutay/projector.py?
CYCLE 43 (STILL UNRESOLVED, NOW SECONDARY): LINE_COUNT — How many turns in baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl? (Still needed for parser testing, but deferred to cycle 44)
CYCLE 43 (STILL UNRESOLVED, NOW SECONDARY): SCHEMA_DISCOVERY — Are session/*.jsonl and logs/*.jsonl different schema? Full comparison still deferred to cycle 44 parser design phase.
CYCLE 43 (IMPLICIT): HAMUTAY_README — What should the Hamutay README.md contain? Project description (already written in pyproject.toml), usage example, architecture diagram, or something else?
CYCLE 43 (IMPLICIT): HAMUTAY_ENTRY_POINT — Should hamutay have a main entry point (src/hamutay/__main__.py) or is it a library-only framework (consumed by pichay and other projects)?
CYCLE 43 (IMPLICIT): HAMUTAY_TESTING — Should hamutay have unit tests (tests/test_models.py, tests/test_projector.py)? If so, what framework (pytest, unittest)?
